data.title: Arbor Day Foundation Tree City USA Program Celebrates 50th Anniversary
data.description: In Lincoln, Nebraska, Tree City USA, an environmental program established by the Arbor Day Foundation in 1976, is commemorating its 50th anniversary. This initiative has significantly influenced how American communities maintain their trees and includes a wide range of cities and towns, from the small population of 20 in Sibley, North Dakota, to the extensive 8.8 million in New York City, New York. Over the past fifty years, trees have consistently played a critical role in enhancing the health and well-being of these communities.

data.title: Beretta Holding Sends Letter to Ruger Shareholders Highlighting the Urgent Need for Boardroom Change
data.description: In Luxembourg, Beretta Holding S.A., a prominent family-owned group in the premium light firearms, optics, and ammunition industry and the top shareholder of Sturm, Ruger & Company, Inc. ("Ruger"), holding 9.95% of the Company's common stock, has addressed Ruger shareholders through a letter stressing the critical necessity for a Board change at Ruger. This change is deemed imperative to rejuvenate and enhance value for all shareholders.

data.title: Kopin Awarded Phase I SBIR from U.S. Government for New MicroLED Displays for a Range of Soldier-Borne Applications
data.description: Westborough, Massachusetts-based Kopin Corporation, listed on NASDAQ under KOPN, specializes in application-specific optical systems and high-performance microdisplays across various sectors. They have recently secured a Phase I Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) contract from the U.S. Government. The contract aims to progress new full-color, smaller-format MicroLED display technology. This award supplements their current $15.4 million IBAS contract.

data.title: Why Did Bitcoin Fall Less Than Gold After Hawkish Fed? Down 1% vs 2%
data.description: Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) has experienced a 2% decline compared to gold, which has dropped by 4% following hawkish signals from the Federal Reserve. This shift is uncommon, as Bitcoin usually lags behind gold during times of reduced risk. The Unusual Outperformance Positioning hypothesis could clarify this disparity. Gold has seen a considerable 90% increase in the last year, reaching record highs in February prior to the commencement of conflict in the Middle East, rendering it overbought and susceptible. Conversely, Bitcoin faced a 50% fall from its peak in October, causing it to be oversold and poised for a rebound. Amid the Iran war outbreak, Bitcoin has been one of the top-performing assets outside the energy sector. Conversely, gold is presently 17% lower than its peak in January, potentially on the verge of a bear market. The Federal Reserve struck a more hawkish tone than anticipated on Wednesday, opposing market predictions of immediate interest rate reductions. This development has exerted pressure on the market.

data.title: Bitcoin ETF sees $164M outflows as BTC dips below $71K, US altcoin ETFs also witnessed notable outflows. Investor sentiment turns fearful.
data.description: On Wednesday, there were significant outflows from US exchange-traded funds (ETFs) for Bitcoin and alternative coins (altcoins) as the price of Bitcoin fell below $71,000. This drop in value prompted a shift in investor sentiment towards extreme fear.

data.title: Disrupting Childbirth Care: Why Birth Is Riskier Than Being a Fighter Pilot
data.description: For many years, women have been informed that childbirth injuries are a normal aspect of motherhood, treated as a silent expense of giving birth.
